# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI / CD Release Workflow
on: 
  tag_push:
    types: [created] # This will only fire when a new commit is pushed, not on existing tags or branches (i.e., it's the trigger for versioning)
jobs:
   build-and-publish:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest 
     
     steps:   
       - name: Checkout code # This action checks out your repository so you can use GitHub Actions features like runners and environment variables.       
         uses: actions/checkout@v2  
         
       - name: Setup NodeJS Environment (14) 
         uses: actions/setup-node@v1    # This action sets up the node version in your repository, so you can use npm packages or build tools.       
         with:            
           node-version: '14'  
           
       - name: Install Dependencies 
         run: |         
           cd myapp && yarn install --frozen-lockfile    # replace "myapp" by the actual path to your project.       
     
       - name: Build Application (production)    
         env:           
             NODE_ENV:' production' 
         run : |         
           cd myapp && yarn build   # Replace 'myapp' with实际的项目路径。   
             
       - name: Publish to NPM/PyPI     
         if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags')     # This will only run when a tag is pushed (i.e., it triggers releases)       
         env:           
             NODE_AUTH_TOKEN : ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}  # Replace NPM_TOKEN by the actual token you have in your repository's secret settings      
         run: |         
           cd myapp && yarn publish    # Publish to npm/PyPI
